packets.body:
## Identity

## Edges

- FIGHTS_IN: Rape of the Three Sisters (track_b: Battles)
- DEFEATS: House Arryn (track_b: Result)

## Origins

The war was the result of the [Rape of the Three Sisters](wiki:Rape_of_the_Three_Sisters) two thousand years ago, when [northmen](wiki:Northmen) occupied the previously-independent islands known as the [Three Sisters](wiki:Three_Sisters). In response, the [pirate](wiki:Pirate) kings of the [Sistermen](wiki:Sistermen) bent the knee to King [Mathos II Arryn](wiki:Mathos_II_Arryn) in exchange for the [Vale of Arryn](wiki:Vale_of_Arryn)'s assistance in driving out the northmen.(wiki:War_Across_the_Water.cite_ref-Radwd9.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0)(wiki:War_Across_the_Water.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_vale.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1)

Mathos sailed for [Sisterton](wiki:Sisterton) with a hundred warships. Although he never returned from war, his sons and descendants continued the fight against the northmen for a thousand years.(wiki:War_Across_the_Water.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_vale.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)

Control of the Three Sisters changed more than twelve times. Northmen landed on the [Fingers](wiki:Fingers) three times;(wiki:War_Across_the_Water.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_vale.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3) King [Theon Stark](wiki:Theon_Stark) once conquered the islands and landed an army on the Fingers,(wiki:War_Across_the_Water.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_north:_the_kings_of_winter.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) though it is unknown if this was part of the Worthless War or during a different time. A northern castle, the [Wolf's Den](wiki:Wolf's_Den) at the mouth of the [White Knife](wiki:White_Knife), was besieged by King [Osgood Arryn](wiki:Osgood_Arryn) and burned by his son, [Oswin the Talon](wiki:Oswin_Arryn).(wiki:War_Across_the_Water.cite_ref-Radwd29.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) The [Starks](wiki:House_Stark) attacked [Gulltown](wiki:Gulltown) in response and, unable to overcome its walls, burned hundreds of ships in the city's harbor.(wiki:War_Across_the_Water.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_vale.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4)

The war eventually ended when House Stark simply lost interest in controlling the Three Sisters.(wiki:War_Across_the_Water.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_vale.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-5)

## Aftermath

The war is described in Archmaester [Perestan](wiki:Perestan)'s *[A Consideration of History](wiki:A_Consideration_of_History)*.(wiki:War_Across_the_Water.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_vale.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-6)

Rule by the triumphant [Arryns](wiki:House_Arryn) over the [Three Sisters](wiki:Three_Sisters) has remained tenuous.(wiki:War_Across_the_Water.cite_ref-Radwd9.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1) Some [Sistermen](wiki:Sistermen) still resent [northmen](wiki:Northmen) for the invasion.(wiki:War_Across_the_Water.cite_ref-Radwd9.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2)

## Quotes

> When his lady wife questioned the wisdom of involving the [Vale](wiki:Vale_of_Arryn) in this War Across the Water, [His Grace](wiki:Mathos_II_Arryn) famously replied that he would sooner have [a pirate](wiki:Three_Sisters) than [a wolf](wiki:House_Stark) for his neighbor.(wiki:War_Across_the_Water.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_vale.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-7)
>
> —writings of [Yandel](wiki:Yandel)

> This was not a case of the [Eyrie](wiki:Eyrie) winning so much as [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell) losing interest. For ten long centuries [the direwolf](wiki:House_Stark) and [the falcon](wiki:House_Arryn) had fought and bled over [three rocks](wiki:Three_Sisters), until one day the wolf awoke as from a dream and realized it was only stone between his teeth, whence he spat it out and walked away.(wiki:War_Across_the_Water.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_vale.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-8)
>
> —[Perestan](wiki:Perestan) in [A Consideration of History](wiki:A_Consideration_of_History)

> [The wolf](wiki:House_Stark) and [the falcon](wiki:House_Arryn) fought over us for a thousand years, till between the two of them they had gnawed all the fat and flesh off the bones of [these poor islands](wiki:Three_Sisters).(wiki:War_Across_the_Water.cite_ref-Radwd9.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-3)
>
> —[Godric Borrell](wiki:Godric_Borrell) to [Davos Seaworth](wiki:Davos_Seaworth)

## Identity

## Edges

- FIGHTS_IN: War of the Five Kings (track_b: Conflict)
- DEFEATS: House Blackwood (track_b: Result)

## Origins

After the [Red Wedding](wiki:Red_Wedding), most of the river lords, such as Lord [Jonos Bracken](wiki:Jonos_Bracken), choose to submit to the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne). However, Lord [Tytos Blackwood](wiki:Tytos_Blackwood) resists for honor's sake. Jonos is persuaded by Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) to attack Tytos's seat, [Raventree Hall](wiki:Raventree_Hall), in return for lands historically claimed by [Stone Hedge](wiki:Stone_Hedge).(wiki:Siege_of_Raventree.cite_ref-Rasos53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)(wiki:Siege_of_Raventree.cite_ref-Rasos74.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)(wiki:Siege_of_Raventree.cite_ref-Radwd48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)

## Narrative Arc

The rivalry between the Blackwoods and Brackens goes back thousands of years so neither side asks or offers quarter. While there are skirmishes at the start of the siege, it develops into a stalemate. Jonos lacks siege towers, battering rams, and catapults, preferring instead to starve out Tytos.(wiki:Siege_of_Raventree.cite_ref-Radwd48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3) Young [Robert Blackwood](wiki:Robert_Blackwood) dies from a looseness of the bowels.(wiki:Siege_of_Raventree.cite_ref-Radwd48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4)

After resolving the [siege of Riverrun](wiki:Siege_of_Riverrun), Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) is asked by Lord [Karyl Vance](wiki:Karyl_Vance) to assist in the resolution of the siege of Raventree, as Tytos will never yield to Jonos. Ser [Daven Lannister](wiki:Daven_Lannister) advises Jaime to bring the siege equipment at [Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun) to Raventree, but Jaime is confident he can resolve the conflict personally and has the equipment put to the torch.(wiki:Siege_of_Raventree.cite_ref-Raffc44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1)

## Aftermath

The surrender of Raventree represents the last remnant of [Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark)'s brief [river kingdom](wiki:King_of_the_Trident), as the entire nobility of the riverlands have now submitted to the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne). After resolving the siege, Jaime travels to [Pennytree](wiki:Pennytree).(wiki:Siege_of_Raventree.cite_ref-Radwd48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-7)

## Quotes

> I mean to deal with Lord [Tytos](wiki:Tytos_Blackwood) myself. It won't require a siege tower.(wiki:Siege_of_Raventree.cite_ref-Raffc44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2)
>
> —[Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) to [Daven Lannister](wiki:Daven_Lannister)

> Lord Jaime, you must go to [Raventree](wiki:Raventree_Hall). So long as it is [Jonos](wiki:Jonos_Bracken) at his gates [Tytos](wiki:Tytos_Blackwood) will never yield, but I know he will bend his knee for you.(wiki:Siege_of_Raventree.cite_ref-Raffc44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-3)
>
> —[Karyl Vance](wiki:Karyl_Vance) to [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister)

> No doubt there had been sorties and skirmishes at the start of the siege, and arrows flying back and forth; half a year into it, everyone was too tired for such nonsense. Boredom and routine had taken over, the enemies of discipline.(wiki:Siege_of_Raventree.cite_ref-Radwd48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-8)
>
> —thoughts of [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister)

## Identity

## Edges

- FIGHTS_IN: Conflict Beyond the Wall (track_b: Conflict)
- DEFEATS: Night's Watch (track_b: Result)

## Origins

[Mance Rayder](wiki:Mance_Rayder), the [King-Beyond-the-Wall](wiki:King-Beyond-the-Wall), leads the gathered [free folk](wiki:Free_folk) south, hoping to lead them through the gates at [Castle Black](wiki:Castle_Black) after having learned that the bulk of the fighting force of the [Night's Watch](wiki:Night's_Watch) has been wiped out in the [fight at the Fist](wiki:Fight_at_the_Fist) by the [Others](wiki:Others). In order to do so, he first plans to weaken the strength of the brothers of the Night's Watch who still remain at the castle.(wiki:Fight_at_the_Bridge_of_Skulls.cite_ref-Rasos48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)(wiki:Fight_at_the_Bridge_of_Skulls.cite_ref-Rasos73.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)

Mance sends several small raiding parties to attack various points along the [Wall](wiki:Wall). This prompts [Bowen Marsh](wiki:Bowen_Marsh), the [Lord Steward](wiki:Lord_Steward) and [castellan](wiki:Castellan) of Castle Black, to march with the garrison, taking most of the fighting men still remaining there. Bowen leaves only the old, the green, and the sickly behind to defend Castle Black.(wiki:Fight_at_the_Bridge_of_Skulls.cite_ref-Rasos48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1)

Bowen chases wildlings to the [Shadow Tower](wiki:Shadow_Tower) and beyond, into the [Gorge](wiki:Gorge).(wiki:Fight_at_the_Bridge_of_Skulls.cite_ref-Rasos69.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2) As Castle Black prepares for the [attack from the south](wiki:Attack_on_Castle_Black) by [Styr](wiki:Styr) and his men, [Donal Noye](wiki:Donal_Noye) sends riders after Bowen to fetch them back.(wiki:Fight_at_the_Bridge_of_Skulls.cite_ref-Rasos55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)

## Narrative Arc

When the [Weeper](wiki:Weeper) tries to cut his way across the [Gorge](wiki:Gorge)(wiki:Fight_at_the_Bridge_of_Skulls.cite_ref-Radwd53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0) with three hundred [free folk](wiki:Free_folk) raiders,(wiki:Fight_at_the_Bridge_of_Skulls.cite_ref-Rasos69.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-3) [Bowen Marsh](wiki:Bowen_Marsh) and the [Night's Watch](wiki:Night's_Watch) confront them on the [Bridge of Skulls](wiki:Bridge_of_Skulls). It is a bloody battle, with more than a hundred brothers of the Watch slain in the engagement, among them Ser [Endrew Tarth](wiki:Endrew_Tarth) and Ser [Aladale Wynch](wiki:Aladale_Wynch). Bowen is seriously wounded in the battle.(wiki:Fight_at_the_Bridge_of_Skulls.cite_ref-Rasos69.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-4)

## Aftermath

The wounded [Bowen Marsh](wiki:Bowen_Marsh) has to be carried back to the [Shadow Tower](wiki:Shadow_Tower). Ser [Denys Mallister](wiki:Denys_Mallister), the Commander of the Shadow Tower, sends a [raven](wiki:Raven) to [Castle Black](wiki:Castle_Black) to inform its defenders that it will take some time before Bowen is fit enough to return. This news arrives as Castle Black is [under assault](wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall) from the main wildling force of [Mance Rayder](wiki:Mance_Rayder).(wiki:Fight_at_the_Bridge_of_Skulls.cite_ref-Rasos69.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-5)

When Denys rides east toward Castle Black with Bowen and other survivors from the [Bridge of Skulls](wiki:Bridge_of_Skulls), their column encounters [Samwell Tarly](wiki:Samwell_Tarly) and [Gilly](wiki:Gilly) beneath the Wall.(wiki:Fight_at_the_Bridge_of_Skulls.cite_ref-Rasos75.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)

The [Weeper](wiki:Weeper) survives the Bridge of Skulls and remains [beyond the Wall](wiki:Beyond_the_Wall). [Tormund](wiki:Tormund) later believes that the Weeping Man will attempt to cross the bridge again.(wiki:Fight_at_the_Bridge_of_Skulls.cite_ref-Radwd53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-1)

## Quotes

> **Edd**: The gods always smiled on [Watt](wiki:Watt_of_Long_Lake), though. When the [wildlings](wiki:Free_folk) knocked him off the [Bridge of Skulls](wiki:Bridge_of_Skulls), somehow he landed in a nice deep pool of water. How lucky was that, missing all those rocks?
>
> **Grenn**: Was it a long fall? Did landing in the pool of water save his life?
>
> —[Eddison Tollett](wiki:Eddison_Tollett) and [Grenn](wiki:Grenn)

> We bled to stop the wildlings at the [Gorge](wiki:Gorge). Good men were slain there, friends and brothers.(wiki:Fight_at_the_Bridge_of_Skulls.cite_ref-Radwd10.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0)
>
> —[Bowen Marsh](wiki:Bowen_Marsh) to [Jon Snow](wiki:Jon_Snow)

## Identity

## Edges

- FIGHTS_IN: War of the Five Kings (track_b: Conflict)
- DEFEATS: Brotherhood (track_b: Result)

## Origins

A detachment of [Brave Companions](wiki:Brave_Companions), a [sellsword](wiki:Sellsword) company pejoratively called the Bloody Mummers, occupy a settlement in the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands) containing [a septry](wiki:Burning_septry), mill, brewhouse, and stables. The septry by a small river had previously been looted by other combatants, including [Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister) troops.(wiki:Battle_at_the_burning_septry.cite_ref-Rasos39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-0)

The lightning lord, [Beric Dondarrion](wiki:Beric_Dondarrion), leads the [outlaw](wiki:Outlaw) [brotherhood without banners](wiki:Brotherhood_without_banners) in reprisals against those who harm [smallfolk](wiki:Smallfolk).

## Narrative Arc

Guarded by [Beardless Dick](wiki:Beardless_Dick) and [Mudge](wiki:Mudge_(brotherhood)), [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark) and [Gendry](wiki:Gendry) observe the battle from a forested ridge overlooking the septry.

The archers [Anguy](wiki:Anguy), [Kyle](wiki:Kyle_(brotherhood)), and [Notch](wiki:Notch) begin the fighting by slaying three Mummer guards shortly before dawn. The Brave Companions inside the septry are multinational, including a [Myrish](wiki:Myr) crossbowman, two [Ibbenese](wiki:Ibben), [Dothraki](wiki:Dothraki), and [Volantenes](wiki:Volantis), among others. When the septry is set ablaze by fire arrows, the Mummers charge forth but are met by [Beric Dondarrion](wiki:Beric_Dondarrion)'s brotherhood of outlaws. There is also fighting in the stables. Although Kyle is killed, he is quickly avenged by [Lem](wiki:Lem). [Thoros of Myr](wiki:Thoros_of_Myr) and Beric both wield flaming swords against the Mummers, and the young Lord [Edric Dayne](wiki:Edric_Dayne) fights by Beric's side. The Brave Companions surrender after a short battle.(wiki:Battle_at_the_burning_septry.cite_ref-Rasos39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1)

## Aftermath

[Jack-Be-Lucky](wiki:Jack-Be-Lucky), [Harwin](wiki:Harwin) and [Merrit o' Moontown](wiki:Merrit_o'_Moontown) rescue eight captive brown brothers from the burning septry before it collapses. The surviving Mummers are taken prisoner, with the exception of two Dothraki whom Beric allows to escape. [Utt](wiki:Utt), the disgraced septon of the Brave Companions, is found hiding beneath the cellar steps. Despite their protests and excuses, Utt and his fellow sellswords are hanged by Beric's outlaws.(wiki:Battle_at_the_burning_septry.cite_ref-Rasos39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)

The rescued brown brothers host Beric's outlaws at the nearby brewhouse that evening, since the septry has been destroyed.(wiki:Battle_at_the_burning_septry.cite_ref-Rasos39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)

## Quotes

> Let them carry the word back to [Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal). It will give the [Leech Lord](wiki:Roose_Bolton) and [his goat](wiki:Vargo_Hoat) a few more sleepless nights.(wiki:Battle_at_the_burning_septry.cite_ref-Rasos39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4)
>
> —[Beric Dondarrion](wiki:Beric_Dondarrion) after letting two Mummers escape

# Wyman Manderly stages the fake execution of Davos Seaworth

Parent event for Wyman Manderly's covert operation to ransom his captive son Wylis from the Iron Throne by making Cersei's small council believe Davos Seaworth has been executed in White Harbor. Wyman calls it "the mummer's farce" (ADWD Davos IV) — a discrete, multi-beat deception staged inside the broader Grand Northern Conspiracy.

The deception has four canonical beats reified as sub-beat nodes:

1. **Public arrest** in the Merman's Court (ADWD Davos III) — `wyman-publicly-arrests-davos-at-white-harbor`. Theatrical, performed for Frey envoys.
2. **Public execution order** (ADWD Davos III) — `wyman-publicly-orders-davos-execution` (renamed S93 from `lord-wyman-orders-execution`). Wyman commands his cousin Ser Marlon Manderly to take Davos to the Wolf's Den, behead him, and return head and hands by supper with an onion in Davos's teeth.
3. **Substitute beheading** (ADWD Davos IV, reported) — `execution-of-davos-lookalike-at-white-harbor`. An unnamed criminal "who resembles Davos" is killed in Davos's place; head and hands are dipped in tar, fingers shortened by Ser Bartimus so the publicly-displayed remains pass for Davos. Davos himself goes to comfortable Wolf's Den captivity, then to Stannis as Wyman's envoy.
4. **Frey witnesses attest to small council** (AFFC Cersei IV) — `frey-witnesses-attest-davos-dead-at-small-council`. Members of House Frey testify that they have seen Davos's head; Cersei is deceived. In return, the Iron Throne returns Wylis Manderly from captivity at Maidenpool — the political payoff.

The deception is the structural pivot of Wyman's Grand Northern Conspiracy arc: it both protects Davos (so he can carry Wyman's terms to Stannis: Manderly fealty in exchange for Rickon Stark's return) and extracts Wylis from Lannister hands. Wyman's line — "the mummer's farce is almost done. My son is home." (ADWD Davos IV) — names the whole operation.

## Edges

Role edges, SUB_BEAT_OF beats, and the CONSPIRES_WITH alliance edge are emitted into `graph/edges/edges.jsonl` as part of the S93 restructure batch.

## Notes

Type `event.deception` is the new entity subtype added Session 93 (2026-06-12) — see `reference/architecture.md:118`. This node is the seed instance; canonical examples in that schema row include this arc plus Cersei's false-attack claim, Theon's burned-boys charade, and the "Arya Stark" (Jeyne Poole) substitution — each of which is a candidate for future event.deception promotion.

## Identity

## Edges

- FIGHTS_IN: Dance of the Dragons (track_b: Conflict)
- DEFEATS: Lucerys Velaryon (track_b: Result)
- DEFEATS: Arrax (track_b: Result)
- DEFEATS: House Baratheon (track_b: Result)
- DEFEATS: greens (track_b: Result)

## Origins

### History

In [120 AC](wiki:120_AC), Prince [Lucerys Velaryon](wiki:Lucerys_Velaryon) confronted his half-uncle, Prince [Aemond Targaryen](wiki:Aemond_Targaryen), together with his brothers [Jacaerys](wiki:Jacaerys_Velaryon) and [Joffrey](wiki:Joffrey_Velaryon) when Aemond claimed the [dragon](wiki:Dragon) [Vhagar](wiki:Vhagar) on [Driftmark](wiki:Driftmark). The three Velaryon siblings attacked Aemond with wooden swords from the training yard. In the fighting, Aemond broke Lucerys's nose and smacked one of the swords against the back of Jacerys's head. As the three younger boys scrambled away, Aemond began to mock them, calling them "the Strongs". Jacaerys, the eldest of the three Velaryon brothers, understood what the insult meant, and attacked Amond once more. Aemond began pummeling Jacaerys savagely. In an attempt to help his brother, Lucerys drew his dagger and slashed Aemond across the face, taking out his right eye.(wiki:Fight_above_Shipbreaker_Bay.cite_ref-Rfabthe_heirs_of_the_dragon_-_a_question_of_succession.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-0)(wiki:Fight_above_Shipbreaker_Bay.cite_ref-Rtrp.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0)

In [129 AC](wiki:129_AC), King [Viserys I Targaryen](wiki:Viserys_I_Targaryen) died abed on the third day of the third month of the year.(wiki:Fight_above_Shipbreaker_Bay.cite_ref-Rtpatq.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) Although Viserys had appointed his daughter [Rhaenyra](wiki:Rhaenyra_Targaryen), his only surviving child from his first [marriage](wiki:Marriage), as his heir, the supporters of his second wife, [Alicent Hightower](wiki:Alicent_Hightower), crowned instead Viserys's eldest son by Alicent, [Aegon II Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_II_Targaryen). Rhaenyra and Aegon fought over the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) over the course of the following two and a half years in the [Dance of the Dragons](wiki:Dance_of_the_Dragons).(wiki:Fight_above_Shipbreaker_Bay.cite_ref-Rtpatq.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)(wiki:Fight_above_Shipbreaker_Bay.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)

During the first few months of the year, both factions began to seek support for their cause. Both Aegon II's [greens](wiki:Greens) and Rhaenyra's [blacks](wiki:Blacks) sought to secure an alliance with [Storm's End](wiki:Storm's_End), ruled by Lord [Borros Baratheon](wiki:Borros_Baratheon). Within days of Viserys I's death, Dowager Queen Alicent sent her second son, [Aemond Targaryen](wiki:Aemond_Targaryen), to Storm's End to propose a betrothal between himself and one of Borros's four daughters, to secure the allegiance. Aemond was uncertain how he would be received, but found a warm welcome at Storm's End, where Borros hosted him feasts, hunts, and jousts. Aemond remained at Storm’s End for a while, during which time Lord Borros, feeling Rhaenyra had taken [House Baratheon](wiki:House_Baratheon) for granted for too long, agreed on a betrothal and gave Aemond a choice from his four daughters.(wiki:Fight_above_Shipbreaker_Bay.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_the_blacks_and_the_greens.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)(wiki:Fight_above_Shipbreaker_Bay.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_a_son_for_a_son.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)

The [blacks](wiki:Blacks) remained unaware of Aemond's presence at the Baratheon stronghold. Princess [Rhaenys Targaryen](wiki:Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aemon)), a supporter of Rhaenyra, believed that Borros would support Rhaenyra, as Rhaenys's uncle and Borros's father, [Boremund](wiki:Boremund_Baratheon), had always been a staunch supporter of Rhaenys and her son [Laenor](wiki:Laenor_Velaryon). Because they believed their envoy would receive a warm welcome, Rhaenyra agreed to send her second son, [Lucerys Velaryon](wiki:Lucerys_Velaryon), to Storm's End as an envoy, to personally deliver a message from her to Lord Borros.(wiki:Fight_above_Shipbreaker_Bay.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_the_blacks_and_the_greens.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-1)

### Confrontation at Storm's End

[Lucerys Velaryon](wiki:Lucerys_Velaryon) arrived at Storm's End before a gathering storm. By the time Lucerys arrived, however, Aemond and Borros were already haggling over dates and dowries. Lucerys found Aemond in the Round Hall, together with Lord Borros, his four daughters [Cassandra](wiki:Cassandra_Baratheon), [Maris](wiki:Maris_Baratheon), [Floris](wiki:Floris_Baratheon), and [Ellyn](wiki:Ellyn_Baratheon), Borros's septon and maester, and twoscore knights, guards, and servants, including Ser [Byron Swann](wiki:Byron_Swann).

Aemond greeted Lucerys with an insult but was ignored. When Lucerys tried to give Rhaenyra's message to Lord Borros, Aemond tried to grasp it and Baratheon's men intervened. Upon learning the contents of the letter, Lord Borros scowled, asking which of his daughters Lucerys would marry, if Borros were to do as Rhaenyra wished of him. When Lucerys admitted that he was not free to marry, Borros send him away.(wiki:Fight_above_Shipbreaker_Bay.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_a_son_for_a_son.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-1) Upon that, Aemond drew his sword, insisting Lucerys put out his own eye to repay him for the eye Aemond had lost years before. Lucerys refused to fight, however, at which Aemond grew more angry. Baratheon's men kept Aemond inside as Lucerys was escorted outside, mounted his dragon, and departed Storm's End.(wiki:Fight_above_Shipbreaker_Bay.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_a_son_for_a_son.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-2)

## Aftermath

Three days after the encounter, the head and neck of [Arrax](wiki:Arrax) washed up beneath the cliffs below [Storm's End](wiki:Storm's_End). According to the dwarf [Mushroom](wiki:Mushroom)'s scurrilous *[Testimony](wiki:The_Testimony_of_Mushroom)*, the body of Lucerys Velaryon washed ashore as well. Mushroom claims that Prince Aemond Targaryen cut out the eyes of the boy and presented them on a bed of seaweed to Maris Baratheon. Other accounts state that Lucerys had been snatched up by Vhagar, who subsequently swallowed him. Yet other accounts claim that Lucerys survived the fall, swam ashore, but suffered from amnesia and lived the rest of his life as a [simpleminded](wiki:Simple) fisherman. In his *[True Telling](wiki:True_Telling)*, [Munkun](wiki:Munkun) claims that Lucerys died with his dragon, a fact which Archmaester Gyldayn agrees with, claiming that Lucerys's body was never found.(wiki:Fight_above_Shipbreaker_Bay.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_a_son_for_a_son.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-5)

For his role in Lucerys's death, Aemond was henceforth known as Aemond the [Kinslayer](wiki:Kinslayer). Aemond did not receive a hero's welcome in King's Landing when he returned with the news. His mother, Dowager Queen [Alicent Hightower](wiki:Alicent_Hightower), paled and cried out for [the Mother](wiki:Mother_(the_Seven))'s mercy. His grandfather and the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), Ser [Otto Hightower](wiki:Otto_Hightower), was also displeased, telling Aemond, *"You only lost one eye, how could you be so blind?"* However, Aemond's brother King [Aegon II Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_II_Targaryen) welcomed him home with a great feast, and announced that he had made *"a good beginning."*(wiki:Fight_above_Shipbreaker_Bay.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_a_son_for_a_son.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-6)

Lucerys's mother, [Rhaenyra Targaryen](wiki:Rhaenyra_Targaryen), collapsed when told of her son's death, and his younger brother Joffrey swore an oath o

## Origins

After [Catelyn Stark](wiki:Catelyn_Stark) takes prisoner [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister) at the [crossroads inn](wiki:Crossroads_inn), Tyrion's father, Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister), retaliates by sending Ser [Gregor Clegane](wiki:Gregor_Clegane) to raid the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands). Without displaying their banners, the [Mountain's men](wiki:Mountain's_men) attack the [Mummer's Ford](wiki:Mummer's_Ford), [Sherrer](wiki:Sherrer), and [Wendish Town](wiki:Wendish_Town).(wiki:Battle_at_the_Mummer's_Ford.cite_ref-Ragot43.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)

Lord [Hoster Tully](wiki:Hoster_Tully), Catelyn's father, responds by sending Ser [Marq Piper](wiki:Marq_Piper), Ser [Karyl Vance](wiki:Karyl_Vance), and Ser [Raymun Darry](wiki:Raymun_Darry) to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) to demand justice for the towns that have been pillaged.(wiki:Battle_at_the_Mummer's_Ford.cite_ref-Ragot43.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1) Tywin hopes that Lord [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark), the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), will personally lead the force which will respond to Gregor, and an ambush is planned so that Tywin can capture Eddard as a hostage to trade for Tyrion.(wiki:Battle_at_the_Mummer's_Ford.cite_ref-Rasos17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2)

However, Eddard's leg is broken by an altercation with Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) in the streets of King's Landing and he cannot lead himself. Lord Stark passes over the impetuous Ser [Loras Tyrell](wiki:Loras_Tyrell), instead granting command to [Beric Dondarrion](wiki:Beric_Dondarrion) and tasking the Lord of [Blackhaven](wiki:Blackhaven) with killing Gregor in the name of King [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon). Beric, [Thoros of Myr](wiki:Thoros_of_Myr), Ser [Gladden Wylde](wiki:Gladden_Wylde), and Lord [Lothar Mallery](wiki:Lothar_Mallery) are each to raise twenty men-at-arms, and Eddard provides twenty [House Stark guards](wiki:House_Stark_guards) (including [Alyn](wiki:Alyn) and [Harwin](wiki:Harwin)) to fight alongside them.(wiki:Battle_at_the_Mummer's_Ford.cite_ref-Ragot43.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-2) Raymun Darry joins the royal party as well.(wiki:Battle_at_the_Mummer's_Ford.cite_ref-Rasos17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-3)

## Narrative Arc

The force under Lord [Beric Dondarrion](wiki:Beric_Dondarrion) is intercepted at the [Mummer's Ford](wiki:Mummer's_Ford) by forces from [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister). They make a fighting retreat only to be taken in the rear by Ser [Gregor Clegane](wiki:Gregor_Clegane) and the [Mountain's men](wiki:Mountain's_men). Gregor kills Ser [Raymun Darry](wiki:Raymun_Darry) and his horse with a single blow, and Lord [Lothar Mallery](wiki:Lothar_Mallery) drowns in the [Red Fork](wiki:Red_Fork). Ser [Gladden Wylde](wiki:Gladden_Wylde) is killed, as are most of Lord [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark)'s men.(wiki:Battle_at_the_Mummer's_Ford.cite_ref-Ragot55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)

Beric is mortally wounded by the Mountain's lance, but his young squire [Edric Dayne](wiki:Edric_Dayne), [Lord of Starfall](wiki:Lord_of_Starfall), pulls him out of the [Red Fork](wiki:Red_Fork) and stands over Beric while the battle rages around them.(wiki:Battle_at_the_Mummer's_Ford.cite_ref-Rasos43.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) [Alyn of Winterfell](wiki:Alyn) rallies the survivors into a retreat. Thoros reflexively says the words of [his faith](wiki:R'hllor) and administers the [last kiss](wiki:Last_kiss) to Beric after he passes, but the young lord unexpectedly comes back to life.(wiki:Battle_at_the_Mummer's_Ford.cite_ref-Rasos39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)

## Aftermath

At [Moat Cailin](wiki:Moat_Cailin), [Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark) tells his mother, [Catelyn Stark](wiki:Catelyn_Stark), that the Lannisters have won victories at the [Golden Tooth](wiki:Battle_in_the_hills_below_the_Golden_Tooth) and the [Mummer's Ford](wiki:Mummer's_Ford), and that Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) has closed off the [kingsroad](wiki:Kingsroad) and is marching north towards [Harrenhal](wiki:Yielding_of_Harrenhal).(wiki:Battle_at_the_Mummer's_Ford.cite_ref-Ragot55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1)

The survivors of Beric's company form the core of the [brotherhood without banners](wiki:Brotherhood_without_banners). Later at the [crossroads inn](wiki:Crossroads_inn), after hearing of the [Whispering Wood](wiki:Battle_in_the_Whispering_Wood) and the [Battle of the Camps](wiki:Battle_of_the_Camps), Tywin tells [Tyrion](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister) and Ser [Kevan Lannister](wiki:Kevan_Lannister) that [Thoros of Myr](wiki:Thoros_of_Myr) and Beric continue to plague their foraging parties.(wiki:Battle_at_the_Mummer's_Ford.cite_ref-Ragot69.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0)

## Quotes

> In the name of [Robert](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon) of the [House Baratheon](wiki:House_Baratheon_of_King's_Landing), the First of his Name, [King of the Andals and the Rhoynar and the First Men](wiki:King_of_the_Andals_and_the_Rhoynar_and_the_First_Men), [Lord of the Seven Kingdoms](wiki:Lord_of_the_Seven_Kingdoms) and [Protector of the Realm](wiki:Protector_of_the_Realm), by the word of Eddard of the [House Stark](wiki:House_Stark), his [Hand](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), I charge you to ride to the [westlands](wiki:Westlands) with all haste, to cross the [Red Fork](wiki:Red_Fork) of the [Trident](wiki:Trident) under the king's flag, and there bring the king's justice to the false knight [Gregor Clegane](wiki:Gregor_Clegane), and to all those who shared in his crimes. I denounce him, and attaint him, and strip him of all rank and titles, of all lands and incomes and holdings, and do sentence him to death. May the gods take pity on his soul.(wiki:Battle_at_the_Mummer's_Ford.cite_ref-Ragot43.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-3)

Six score we’d been that morning. By dark no more than two score were left, and [Lord Beric](wiki:Beric_Dondarrion) was gravely wounded. Thoros drew a foot of lance from his chest that night, and poured boiling [wine](wiki:Wine) into the hole it left.(wiki:Battle_at_the_Mummer's_Ford.cite_ref-Rasos17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-4)

[Robert](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon) is slain, but [his realm](wiki:Seven_Kingdoms) remains. And [we defend her](wiki:Brotherhood_without_banners).(wiki:Battle_at_the_Mummer's_Ford.cite_ref-Rasos34.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0)

## Identity

[Drogon](drogon) carries [Daenerys](daenerys-targaryen) north beyond the Skahazadhan and sets down on a volcanic hill she names Dragonstone, stranding her far out on the Dothraki sea — alone, barefoot (one sandal lost in the flight, the other left at the dragon's cave), dressed in rags, burned, and without supplies. She passes days eating Drogon's scorched leavings and failing to ride him back to Meereen; the wild dragon returns to his lair instead of obeying her. Sick and starving, she walks the grass searching for the river and the road home, reciting "Fire and Blood" to the swaying grass as she fixes on who she is rather than the city she lost. The chapter — and Daenerys's whole published arc — closes when Khal Jhaqo's outriders emerge from the drifting smoke and find her.

## Appearances & Description

> Her clothes were hardly more than rags, and offered little in the way of warmth. One of her sandals had slipped off during her wild flight from Meereen and she had left the other up by Drogon's cave, preferring to go barefoot rather than half-shod. Her tokar and veils she had abandoned in the pit, and her linen undertunic had never been made to withstand the hot days and cold nights of the Dothraki sea. Sweat and grass and dirt had stained it, and Dany had torn a strip off the hem to make a bandage for her shin. I must look a ragged thing, and starved.

— Daenerys destitute on the Dothraki sea, ADWD Daenerys X (`sources/chapters/adwd/adwd-daenerys-10.md:35`)

## Narrative Arc

Ser [Gregor Clegane](wiki:Gregor_Clegane) and the [Mountain's men](wiki:Mountain's_men) burn [Stone Hedge](wiki:Stone_Hedge) and the [Bracken](wiki:House_Bracken) lands under orders from Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister).(wiki:Burning_of_Stone_Hedge.cite_ref-Ragot56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0) The Mountain rapes a daughter of Lord [Jonos Bracken](wiki:Jonos_Bracken) and steals or burns the Bracken harvest.(wiki:Burning_of_Stone_Hedge.cite_ref-Radwd48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) [Harry Rivers](wiki:Harry_Rivers), the Bastard of Bracken, is killed by [Lannisters](wiki:House_Lannister) at some point during the war.(wiki:Burning_of_Stone_Hedge.cite_ref-Rasos34.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)

## Aftermath

Following the [Battle of the Camps](wiki:Battle_of_the_Camps), Jonos travels from ruined Stone Hedge to [Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun).(wiki:Burning_of_Stone_Hedge.cite_ref-Ragot71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) Lord Bracken [liberates his castle](wiki:Liberation_of_Stone_Hedge) from the Lannisters after [Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark) is crowned [King of the Trident](wiki:King_of_the_Trident).(wiki:Burning_of_Stone_Hedge.cite_ref-Rasos1.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)

During the [siege of Raventree](wiki:Siege_of_Raventree) after the [Red Wedding](wiki:Red_Wedding), Jonos requests land and castles from [House Blackwood](wiki:House_Blackwood) as recompense for the damage inflicted by the Mountain's men.(wiki:Burning_of_Stone_Hedge.cite_ref-Radwd48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)

After the burning, [Lady Bracken](wiki:Lady_Bracken) spends most of her time praying with her [septon](wiki:Septon).(wiki:Burning_of_Stone_Hedge.cite_ref-Radwd48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-2)

## Origins

After Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) defeats the [Pipers](wiki:House_Piper) and [Vances](wiki:House_Vance_of_Wayfarer's_Rest) in a [battle in the hills below the Golden Tooth](wiki:Battle_in_the_hills_below_the_Golden_Tooth), Lord [Clement Piper](wiki:Clement_Piper) falls back to [Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun).(wiki:Battle_under_the_walls_of_Riverrun.cite_ref-Ragot55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) Jaime and his father, Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister), invade the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands) with separate hosts. Jaime marches directly on Riverrun, where Ser [Edmure Tully](wiki:Edmure_Tully) has assembled many of his father [Hoster Tully](wiki:Hoster_Tully)'s bannermen and knights. Edmure's force is diminished on account of the men he has sent to guard the borders and the [Golden Tooth](wiki:Golden_Tooth) in response to Lannister raiding. The forces of Lord [Walder Frey](wiki:Walder_Frey) are also not present.

## Narrative Arc

Jaime meets the Tullys under the walls of Riverrun. Securing a victory, Jaime puts the [rivermen](wiki:Rivermen) to rout. Edmure and many of his knights and bannermen are taken captive. Lord [Tytos Blackwood](wiki:Tytos_Blackwood) manages to lead some of the surviving men back within Riverrun, where Jaime places them under siege.(wiki:Battle_under_the_walls_of_Riverrun.cite_ref-Ragot56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)

## Aftermath

Tywin leads a second, larger Lannister army against the companies of men Edmure had dispersed along his borders to guard against Ser [Gregor Clegane](wiki:Gregor_Clegane)'s raiding. Tywin and Ser [Kevan Lannister](wiki:Kevan_Lannister) are able to destroy them piecemeal before they can regroup. They then attack several castles of the river lords, including [Raventree Hall](wiki:Fall_of_Raventree_Hall), [Pinkmaiden Castle](wiki:Burning_of_Pinkmaiden_Castle), [Stone Hedge](wiki:Burning_of_Stone_Hedge), [Darry](wiki:Capture_of_Darry), and [Harrenhal](wiki:Yielding_of_Harrenhal). The [westermen](wiki:Westermen) assume a position at the [ruby ford](wiki:Ruby_ford), where they can both block any [Stark](wiki:House_Stark) or [Arryn](wiki:House_Arryn) attempt to relieve Riverrun and can maintain contact with [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing).(wiki:Battle_under_the_walls_of_Riverrun.cite_ref-Ragot56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-2)

## Quotes

> Your brother has been covering himself with glory. He smashed the Lords [Vance](wiki:Lord_Vance) and [Piper](wiki:Clement_Piper) at the [Golden Tooth](wiki:Battle_in_the_hills_below_the_Golden_Tooth), and met the massed power of the [Tullys](wiki:House_Tully) under the walls of [Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun). ## Origins

Lord [Roose Bolton](wiki:Roose_Bolton), having decided to switch his allegiance from [House Stark](wiki:House_Stark) to [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister), sends a third of the [northern](wiki:North) foot to [defeat at Duskendale](wiki:Battle_at_Duskendale). Roose leaves Harrenhal to its new lord, [Vargo Hoat](wiki:Vargo_Hoat) of the [Brave Companions](wiki:Brave_Companions), as he goes north to the [Twins](wiki:Twins) for the [Red Wedding](wiki:Red_Wedding) with the remaining northmen. There is [fighting at the Trident](wiki:Fighting_at_the_fords_of_the_Trident) while Roose is on his way to the wedding, as he allows Ser [Gregor Clegane](wiki:Gregor_Clegane) to overwhelm his rearguard at the [ruby ford](wiki:Ruby_ford).(wiki:Capture_of_Harrenhal.cite_ref-Rasos49.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0) Vargo suffers from infection after his ear is bitten off by [Brienne of Tarth](wiki:Brienne_of_Tarth).(wiki:Capture_of_Harrenhal.cite_ref-Rasos44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)

## Aftermath

[Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal) is again in the hands of [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister). Members of the [Brave Companions](wiki:Brave_Companions) are on the run throughout the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands). When Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) returns to Harrenhal after the death of Ser [Gregor Clegane](wiki:Gregor_Clegane), the only servants remaining of those who had once served Lady [Shella Whent](wiki:Shella_Whent) are the maimed cook, [Ben Blackthumb](wiki:Ben_Blackthumb), and [Pia](wiki:Pia).(wiki:Capture_of_Harrenhal.cite_ref-Raffc27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-3)

## Identity

While [Robb Stark](robb-stark) was recovering at the [Crag](crag) from a festering arrow wound taken in [the storming](storming-of-the-crag), the [Greatjon Umber](greatjon-umber) brought him word that [Winterfell](winterfell) had fallen and that his brothers [Bran](bran-stark) and [Rickon](rickon-stark) were dead — slain by [Theon Greyjoy](theon-greyjoy), who had [taken the castle](capture-of-winterfell) and displayed two tarred heads above its gates as proof. The report was **false**: the heads belonged to two miller's boys Theon had killed and passed off as the Stark heirs; Bran and Rickon had escaped. But Robb believed it, and the grief drove him into the arms of [Jeyne Westerling](jeyne-westerling), who nursed and comforted him. He [wed her the next day](robb-weds-jeyne-westerling), holding it "the only honorable thing to do" — and in doing so broke his betrothal-pact with [House Frey](house-frey), the breach that would ripen into the [Red Wedding](red-wedding).

## Edges

(Causal/role edges live in `graph/edges/edges.jsonl`, S123 WO5K-remainder track / Q5. Caused by [Theon's capture of Winterfell](capture-of-winterfell) and its faked deaths (capture-of-winterfell CAUSES, Tier-2). The grief MOTIVATES [Robb Stark](robb-stark) (Tier-2) and TRIGGERS [Robb's marriage to Jeyne](robb-weds-jeyne-westerling) (Tier-2), which separately is ENABLED by [the storming of the Crag](storming-of-the-crag) putting the wounded Robb in Jeyne's care. The wedding in turn TRIGGERS the [Red Wedding conspiracy](red-wedding-conspiracy) (already wired). The falseness of the news is itself the hinge of dramatic irony: Robb breaks the alliance that kills him over brothers who are alive.)

Bran and Rickon.” He seemed to have trouble saying his brothers’ names. “That night, she . . . she comforted me, Mother.
